name: Emmeralda
description: Elven courtesan of The Sylph's Embrace who survived Santo di Teufel's botched binding ritual. Partially possessed by a lesser pleasure demon -- graceful and charming (CHA 18, Seduction 88) but prone to bestial voices and asking for 'Lord Santo.' Living evidence of Santo's crime.
narrative: Emmeralda is an elven courtesan at The Sylph's Embrace and the victim of Santo di Teufel's brutal, botched binding ritual. Timely healing saved her from complete possession, but a lesser pleasure demon remains partially lodged within her: she suffers strange behaviors, speaks in unsettling bestial voices, and sometimes asks for 'Lord Santo.' By day she is a poised, magnetic performer (Dance 85, Sing 80, Seduction 88); under demonic surge she briefly gains unnatural strength and menace before collapsing. She is both the clearest evidence against Santo and -- because her binding was interrupted rather than completed -- the unique case from which a counter-ritual might be devised. Saving her is a path to undoing the Baron's possessions.

extras.role: Elven courtesan at The Sylph's Embrace who survived Santo di Teufel's botched binding ritual and is partially possessed by a lesser pleasure demon.
extras.possession: Healing interrupted [[santo-di-teufel]]'s ritual before full possession. She suffers strange behaviors, speaks in bestial voices, and occasionally asks for 'Lord Santo.' The demon can surge: brief unnatural strength (treat as STR 16 for one action, claws 1d3) and frightening presence, then she collapses, distraught.
extras.significance: Living evidence of Santo's crime; her interrupted binding is the key to developing a counter-ritual. A potential ally if her possession can be reversed.
extras.magic_tradition: None of her own; the demon-taint is an affliction, not a learned tradition.
extras.source: GM-authored from source lore (The Partially Possessed Courtesan)
